spec_match.rationale: The probe focuses on the quality feature: selected-language availability across quality tiers, processLocally, local pack status, the instance quality property, and start/abort behavior.

interactive.rationale: Run, reset, language, local-only, quality-tier, and start/abort controls all update cards, logs, the matrix evidence, and the result table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now names the selected BCP 47 language and every capability the page actually inspects.

edge_cases.rationale: The page separates unavailable/downloadable packs, absent instance quality, microphone denial, secure context, missing APIs, and trial errors by language and tier.

references.rationale: The page links ChromeStatus feature 5136859632107520, the available(options) spec, and Chrome 150 release material.

conformance.rationale: The immutable feature suite passes all 6 assertions in this browser.

runtime_evidence.rationale: DevTools MCP selected en-US, ran the full probe, and observed command=downloadable, dictation/conversation=unavailable, instance quality absent, microphone denied, secure context yes, with no uncaught errors.

telemetry_health.rationale: Expected capability failures remain structured UI/log results and the console stays clean.

design_system.rationale: The added language selector uses the existing control system and all badges retain text labels alongside color.

accessibility.rationale: Native named selects, checkbox, buttons, labelled result table, and textual cards expose the same capability information as the visual badges.

summary: The old probe tested unspokenPunctuation, a separate Chrome 151 feature, and then described its absence as a Chrome 150 quality failure. That probe is now replaced by the relevant SpeechRecognition.quality check. Users choose the exact language being tested, and every availability/trial result includes its BCP 47 tag and quality tier.
